name: SCO Charter 2002 (Shanghai Cooperation Organisation)
summary: The Charter of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO Charter) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-SCO-treaty framework adopted by the 6 founding Eurasian states in Saint Petersburg — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Eurasian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal Eurasian-strategic-framework + China-Russia-co-anchor framework (SCO Charter signed 7 June 2002 in Saint Petersburg Russia by China + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Russia + Tajikistan + Uzbekistan + effective 19 September 2003 + Shanghai Five predecessor founded 26 April 1996 in Shanghai China — formal foundational SCO treaty framework establishing comprehensive SCO organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-treaty frameworks which operate on bilateral-only frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Eurasian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Eurasian-strategic-framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-Russia-co-anchor framework + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~3.4 billion population coverage framework spanning Eurasia geographic framework after India + Pakistan accession 2017), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Three Evils framework (SCO Charter Article 1 framework providing formal SCO mandate on Three Evils framework: terrorism + separatism + extremism — formal foundational Three-Evils framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three-Evils framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal SCO Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure RATS framework (SCO Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure RATS established 2004 in Tashkent Uzbekistan providing formal SCO counter-terrorism framework — formal foundational SCO counter-terrorism framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional anti-terrorist structure framework with operational counter-terrorism database framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal SCO Membership Expansion framework (India + Pakistan joined SCO 9 June 2017 at Astana Summit + Iran joined SCO 4 July 2023 at Tehran Summit + Belarus joined SCO 4 July 2024 at Astana Summit expanding SCO from original 6 to 10 member states with population coverage framework expanding from ~1.5 billion to ~3.4 billion framework — formal foundational SCO expansion framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal three-stage-expansion framework from 6 founding to 8 with India-Pakistan to 9 with Iran to 10 with Belarus framework). The SCO Charter is binding upon 10 SCO member states + 2 SCO observer states + 14 SCO dialogue partners as of 2026 (Belarus + China + India + Iran + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Pakistan + Russia + Tajikistan + Uzbekistan) — comprehensive Eurasian regional coverage framework. The Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal SCO Three Evils framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Eurasian-regional security framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The SCO Heads of State Council + SCO Heads of Government Council + SCO Secretariat + SCO RATS Executive Committee are the formal SCO bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The SCO Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: SCO Charter
statutory_framework.relevance: Charter of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation signed 7 June 2002 in Saint Petersburg Russia + effective 19 September 2003 — formal foundational SCO treaty framework establishing comprehensive SCO organisation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Shanghai Convention on Combating Terrorism Separatism Extremism 2001
statutory_framework.title: Shanghai Convention
statutory_framework.relevance: Shanghai Convention on Combating Terrorism, Separatism and Extremism signed 15 June 2001 in Shanghai China + effective 29 March 2003 — formal foundational Three-Evils framework.

statutory_framework.title: SCO RATS Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: Agreement on Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure RATS adopted 2002 + RATS operational 2004 in Tashkent Uzbekistan — formal foundational SCO counter-terrorism framework.

statutory_framework.citation: SCO Treaty on Long-Term Good Neighbourliness 2007
statutory_framework.title: SCO Treaty on Long-Term Good Neighbourliness
statutory_framework.relevance: Treaty on Long-Term Good Neighbourliness, Friendship and Cooperation between the Member States of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation signed 16 August 2007 in Bishkek Kyrgyzstan + effective 13 August 2008 — formal foundational SCO good-neighbourliness framework.

apex_courts.name: SCO Heads of State Council
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for SCO.

apex_courts.name: SCO Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure (RATS) Executive Committee

anonymisation_convention: SCO-related communications operate via SCO Heads of State Council framework; individual communications not formally accepted under SCO framework.

key_developments.title: Shanghai Five established
key_developments.description: Shanghai Five founded 26 April 1996 in Shanghai China by China + Kazakhstan + Kyrgyzstan + Russia + Tajikistan — predecessor framework to SCO Charter.

key_developments.title: SCO founded + Shanghai Convention
key_developments.description: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation SCO founded 15 June 2001 in Shanghai China by Shanghai Five + Uzbekistan + Shanghai Convention on Combating Terrorism, Separatism and Extremism signed 15 June 2001 — formal foundational SCO + Three-Evils framework operational.

key_developments.title: SCO Charter adopted
key_developments.description: Charter of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation signed 7 June 2002 in Saint Petersburg Russia — formal foundational SCO treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: SCO Charter enters force
key_developments.description: SCO Charter entered force 19 September 2003 — formal foundational SCO treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: SCO Secretariat + RATS established
key_developments.description: SCO Secretariat established in Beijing China 15 January 2004 + SCO Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure RATS operational 2004 in Tashkent Uzbekistan — formal foundational SCO + RATS frameworks operational.

key_developments.title: SCO Treaty on Long-Term Good Neighbourliness
key_developments.description: Treaty on Long-Term Good Neighbourliness, Friendship and Cooperation signed 16 August 2007 in Bishkek Kyrgyzstan — formal foundational SCO good-neighbourliness framework.

key_developments.title: India + Pakistan join SCO
key_developments.description: India + Pakistan joined SCO 9 June 2017 at Astana Summit — formal SCO expansion framework to 8 member states.

key_developments.title: Iran joins SCO
key_developments.description: Iran joined SCO 4 July 2023 at Tehran Summit — formal SCO expansion framework to 9 member states.

key_developments.title: Belarus joins SCO
key_developments.description: Belarus joined SCO 4 July 2024 at Astana Summit — formal SCO expansion framework to 10 member states.

key_developments.title: Tianjin Summit + SCO 25th anniversary
key_developments.description: SCO 25th anniversary + 25th SCO Heads of State Council Summit held 31 August - 1 September 2025 in Tianjin China — formal SCO 25th anniversary framework.

structural_findings: SCO Charter operates a regional supranational binding-treaty framework — places SCO Charter in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Eurasian regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal Eurasian-strategic-framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-Russia-co-anchor framework + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~3.4 billion population coverage framework spanning Eurasia geographic framework after India + Pakistan accession 2017.

Central jurisdiction of formal Three Evils framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Three-Evils framework: terrorism + separatism + extremism.

Central jurisdiction of formal SCO RATS framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional anti-terrorist structure framework with operational counter-terrorism database framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal SCO Membership Expansion framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal three-stage-expansion framework from 6 founding to 8 with India-Pakistan to 9 with Iran to 10 with Belarus framework.
10 SCO member state + 2 SCO observer state + 14 SCO dialogue partner ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Eurasian regional coverage framework.

1996 Shanghai Five + 2001 SCO founded + Shanghai Convention + 2002 SCO Charter + 2003 entry into force + 2004 Secretariat + RATS + 2007 Good Neighbourliness Treaty + 2017 India + Pakistan + 2023 Iran + 2024 Belarus + 2025 Tianjin Summit SCO-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

SCO Charter + Shanghai Convention + RATS Agreement + Good Neighbourliness Treaty formal quartet framework operating limited PA-intersection framework via Eurasian-regional security framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in NOT operating PA-intersection framework relative to other regional frameworks.
editorial_notes: SCO Charter jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational Eurasian foundational-SCO-treaty meta-framework (SCO Charter 2002/2003 + Shanghai Convention 2001/2003 + RATS Agreement 2002/2004 + Good Neighbourliness Treaty 2007/2008 + Shanghai Five 1996 predecessor + 2017 India + Pakistan + 2023 Iran + 2024 Belarus + 2025 Tianjin Summit). Only modern Eurasian regional supranational binding-treaty framework globally + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal China-Russia-co-anchor framework + most-populous regional supranational binding-treaty framework with ~3.4 billion population coverage spanning Eurasia + central jurisdiction of formal Three-Evils framework: terrorism + separatism + extremism + central jurisdiction of formal SCO RATS counter-terrorism framework + central jurisdiction of formal three-stage SCO expansion framework + 10 SCO member states + 2 observer states + 14 dialogue partners + 1996 Shanghai Five + 2001 SCO founded + 2002 SCO Charter + 2003 entry into force + 2004 Secretariat + RATS + 2007 Good Neighbourliness Treaty + 2017 India + Pakistan + 2023 Iran + 2024 Belarus + 2025 Tianjin Summit SCO-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: SCO Three Evils framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Eurasian-regional security framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in NOT operating PA-intersection framework relative to other regional frameworks.

Joins regional + supranational + Eurasian-foundational-SCO-treaty cluster + SCO-Charter-only-Eurasian-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-globally-distinctive + China-Russia-co-anchor + most-populous-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-3.4-billion + Three-Evils-terrorism-separatism-extremism + SCO-RATS-Tashkent + SCO-Secretariat-Beijing + Shanghai-Five-1996-predecessor + 10-SCO-member-states + 2-SCO-observer-states + 14-SCO-dialogue-partners + 2017-India-Pakistan-Astana-Summit + 2023-Iran-Tehran-Summit + 2024-Belarus-Astana-Summit + 2025-Tianjin-Summit-25th-anniversary + Shanghai-Convention-2001 + Good-Neighbourliness-Treaty-2007 + limited-PA-intersection-via-Eurasian-security-framework-distinctive-NOT-operating-PA-intersection clusters within the corpus.
